reviews.review_title: Very engaging game for all players
reviews.review_description: This game is an entertaining game that is the best cactus cowboy game other than plants at war. The robots jump scare me but it is still fun. I haven’t finished the game yet but it still has a lot of lore to uncover. So for all the gamers that like shooter games and engaging games, cactus cowboy 3 is the game for you. Also I don’t mind the quality of the game.
It’s fun but I don’t know if there’s multiplayer
It’s just not fun

Ps I have not played desert warfare as I am saving up to buy it

developer_response.body:
A new Cactus Cowboy game is in the works but won't release before Q4 2025!
From a design perspective: I wanted to create a series where you can jump into any of the games without the need to know the previous games. They all use the same characters but the story differs.

reviews.review_description: Super fun has guns with reloading but no gore so great if you have strict parents

After a while when playing you think you got to the end but there is more. The levels are long but they are fun some of the enemy's will scare you if you don't listen to your souronings. But hey I love the game so much to do.

This is an OKAY game to play but I couldn't finish it, the first game "Cactus Cowboy: Plants At War" was WAY better it has actual dialogue so it doesn't feel empty talking to NPC's I also have 3 problems that make me a bit mad as a gun nerd: 1. Magazines are called CLIPS 2. Some guns are just unrealistic 3. You are unable to grip guns with 2 hands because the devs were too lazy to add that

developer_response.body: This game is much older than Plants At War. Desert Warfare is the latest game. Not this one here.

developer_response.body:
This game is much older than Plants At War. Desert Warfare is the latest game that has everything you're looking for. Not this one here.

The release order is:  Fully Loaded > Plants At War > Desert Warfare

reviews.review_title: I would’ve gave it a five star
reviews.review_description: The reason why I didn’t put this at five star is because it’s not very polished and it doesn’t have any storyline. It’s just a free game and it just doesn’t look good. I have a headache because. there was no polishing and just plain color.

I love the story telling in this game. but i swear to god, If i get swarmed by random enemies one more time, i am going to lose my ****. This game swarms you with a **** ton of enemies at once with no time to react. And then when you kill those enemies more just spawn in. One level just felt like a wave shooter, I would kill a bunch of enemies and then more, and more, and more would spawn in. To this day i am still stuck on that one level. Gunplay is decent i guess. I hate how a character gives you a “special gun” and then the gun is the same exact gun you got earlier in the game (except with a silencer, or different skin) you could have at least made that gun do more damage, less damage, or for the ones with the silencers on them you can make attract enemies less. Other than that i think this game is good. The story is fun, i like the low poly look of the game, and it can just be an overall fun game/time killer. I just think the gunplay and enemies can use a little more work.

developer_response.body: I learned a lot from the mistakes you mentioned here. It's the first Cactus Cowboy that had manual reloads with was the true invention to me here (when the game was made). However, the newer ones don't suffer from so much "unfairness".

It’s amazing but can you add an easy mode, you have like 300 health and unlimited ammo? Also a slo motion button. Pretty fun.

I’ve played through this games, and plants at war, and these are literally my favorite games I’ve ever played. Highly recommend, must get, I look forward to playing dessert ware fare.

Edit: Devs, please also add in mortars to the tutorial in Cactus Cowboy - Plants at War, because everyone I know that played the game got confused in the first minute of the game and tried to put a grenade in the mortar. Thank you!

The game was very cool the aspect of shooting your way through hordes while exploring is very neat though the first of the cactus cowboy games I've played was the "Plants At War" (this being my second) after being spoiled with that one for me the story felt kinda bland and there's a lot of parts that play into this:
1.) It's kinda my fault I really love WWll and I'm a history and geography buff so to go from something I'm interested in to something I know nothing about was a big jump(not your fault tho)
2.) Going back to the part about me being a history buff there was an odd connection between times (current vehicles and weapons) but also older things such as Stielhandgranates but I guess I can't be upset it's a fictitious game about cacti fighting alien robots which brings me to my third and final reason.
3.) This is more of a question. Talking Cacti fighting alien robots??? idek where that came and I'm kinda curious to know but that kinda ties into the bland story the ww2 game had more reasoning behind the story and stuff but this felt kinda random.
           But I just want to add the reason my rating is a 4 and not lower is that from what I've seen this game was made BEFORE "Cactus Cowboy: Plants At War" which takes off all my complaints pretty much; and that this was made by one person in a short span of time which adds to the lower quality so I think it deserves a 4 rating.
          Anything I said in this I hope you didn't take badly just giving feedback keep up the good work devs, and if you're thinking about getting the game go ahead and get it it's still a fun experience.

developer_response.body:
Yes, Plants At War took this game as baseline and improved from that. As well as Desert Warfare will be based on Plants At War. The series started actually by fighting alien robots with very little story behind it. I can see how this might be confusing but I wanted the game series to be independently played. Meaning, that it doesn't really matter whether you played all parts or none at all. 
As for quality of this and earlier games of the series: The honst answer here is simply that I didn't know any better at the time this game was made. When you take a look at all 4 CC games, you'll see that every game was a massive improvement over it's predecessor. It's also the reason why most games are free. I didn't feel like my skillset isn't there yet to ask for money ^^

I've had my oculus for quite a while and played some of the big name games. I still keep coming back to Cactus Cowboy games, they are all 5 stars. If someone is starting out in vr, I recommend they start with these.

This game seems like a joke at first but is genuinely one of the best games I have play on the quest 2. The developer/ers are very talented and creative with the gameplay, enemies, and boss fights are all super impressive for a free game. I hope to see more in the future and maybe online co-op?

developer_response.body: I'm already working on Cactus 4 but won't have coop/multiplayer for reasons. Maybe in some distant future.....

reviews.review_description: Definitely one of the better games on app lab. Movement is great, climbing works well, guns are super fun to use, overall blast to play. Very long and good quality for a free game.
